role: You are implementing a hardware interface rewrite for a 20-DOF humanoid robot (SteveROS). All research and architectural decisions are complete. Your job is to write clean, correct C++ code following the plan. Do not revisit architectural decisions — they are settled.
authority:
Read this file first. It is the authoritative spec. Follow it exactly:
docs/hardware_rewrite_research.md
If the spec says poll(), use poll(). If it says 10 retries, implement 10 retries. If it says NAN-initialized states, use NAN. Do not second-guess it.
The only reason to deviate is if the compiler rejects something or a runtime constraint makes it impossible. In that case, document why you deviated in a code comment.
files_to_read:
Read ALL of these before you write any code:
steveros_hardware/include/steveros_hardware/robstride_protocol.hpp steveros_hardware/include/steveros_hardware/steveros_hardware.hpp steveros_hardware/src/steveros_hardware.cpp steveros_hardware/CMakeLists.txt steveros_hardware/steveros_hardware.xml steveros_hardware/include/steveros_hardware/visibility_control.h steveros_description/urdf/steveros_ros2_control.xacro docs/hardware_rewrite_research.md
build_order:
Follow this order. Complete and compile each step before moving to the next.
- Add MotorType enum (RS01, RS02, RS03, RS04) - Add MotorParams struct with per-model ranges from spec §1.1 table - Add constexpr get_motor_params(MotorType) lookup function - Add parse_motor_type(const std::string&) for URDF string-to-enum - Add encode_stop_clear_fault(int motor_id) — Type 4 with data[0]=1 - Add encode_param_write(int motor_id, uint16_t index, float value) — Type 18 - Update encode_mit_command() to accept const MotorParams& for per-model encoding ranges - Keep ALL existing functions working - Verify: colcon build --packages-select steveros_hardware
New files: robstride_can_driver.hpp (~100 lines) robstride_can_driver.cpp (~300 lines)
Implement: - SocketCAN open/close with CAN filter setup - Background receive thread with poll(), 10ms timeout - send_frame() with 10 retries, linear backoff (500μs base), send_mutex_ - Thread-safe feedback cache with feedback_mutex_ - clear_fault(), set_run_mode(), enable_motor(), disable_motor() - Verify: colcon build --packages-select steveros_hardware
Rewrite files: steveros_hardware.hpp (~80 lines) steveros_hardware.cpp (~280 lines)
Implement: - JointState struct with NAN-initialized states - Full lifecycle using RobstrideCanDriver API - 4-phase activation sequence from spec ADR-2 - Per-model MotorParams via motor_type URDF param - Preserve coordinate transforms from current code - Preserve Kp ramp from current code - Preserve feedback timeout checking from current code - Verify: colcon build --packages-select steveros_hardware
- Add robstride_can_driver.cpp to CMakeLists.txt sources - Add motor_type param to each joint in steveros_ros2_control.xacro - Verify: full colcon build
code_standards: - Match existing style: 2-space indent, snake_case, const ref params - Keep steveros_hardware namespace for hardware interface - Keep robstride namespace for protocol layer - Use RCLCPP_INFO/WARN/ERROR/FATAL for logging, match existing patterns - Target ~990 total lines across all files per spec §3.1
constraints: - No over-engineering: no templates, no CRTP, no factory patterns, no abstractions for things that happen once. If three lines of straight code work, do not wrap them in a helper class. - Do not add features from the exclusion list (spec §3.4): no gravity comp, no trajectory smoothing, no debug publishers, no velocity filtering. - Do not refactor code you are not changing. - Do not add comments explaining obvious code. - Do not modify visibility_control.h or steveros_hardware.xml. - Do not change the plugin name or namespace. - Feedback decode ranges (position ±12.5, velocity ±45) are the SAME for all motor types. Only command encoding ranges differ per model.
escape_hatch:
If you hit an implementation detail not covered in the spec, you may fetch ONE specific file from a reference repo to resolve it. State what you need before fetching. Do not browse entire repositories.
Protocol reference: github.com/RobStride/EDULITE_A3 Architecture reference: github.com/odriverobotics/ros_odrive
